senses.definition: relating to the base of the occipital bone in the skull
examples: The basioccipital bone forms part of the skull base.
In anatomy class, we studied the basioccipital region.

senses.definition: the basioccipital bone or part of the skull
examples: The basioccipital connects to the sphenoid.
X-rays revealed issues in the basioccipital.

senses.definition: a point at the base of the skull where the anterior border of the foramen magnum meets the median plane
examples: Anthropologists measure the basion in skull studies.
The basion serves as a key craniometric landmark.

senses.definition: plural of basion, points at the skull base
examples: The study compared basions across populations.
Variations in basions indicate genetic differences.

senses.definition: relating to or characteristic of basophils, a type of white blood cell
examples: Basiophitic granules appear in blood smears.
The cell showed basiophitic properties under microscope.

senses.definition: the basal segment of the eye-stalk in certain crustaceans
examples: The basiophthalmite supports the compound eye.
Dissection revealed the basiophthalmite structure.

senses.definition: having eyes at the base of the antennae in arthropods
examples: The crab is basiophthalmous with stalked eyes.
Basiophthalmous species have unique eye placement.

senses.definition: a surgical instrument for crushing the base of a tumor or bone
examples: Surgeons used a basiotribe in the procedure.
The basiotribe helped remove the bony growth.

senses.definition: surgical crushing of the base of a structure, such as a bone or tumor
examples: Basiotripsy was performed on the skull base.
The technique involves basiotripsy for tumor removal.

senses.definition: chromatin material located at the base of the nucleus in certain cells
examples: Basiparachromatin stains darkly in microscopy.
Studies focus on basiparachromatin distribution.

senses.definition: a substance related to plastin found at the base of certain cellular structures
examples: Basiparaplastin supports cell framework.
Microscopic analysis identified basiparaplastin.

senses.definition: developing or moving from the top toward the base, as in plant growth
examples: Basipetal flowering occurs from apex to base.
The hormone spreads in a basipetal direction.

senses.definition: in a direction from the top toward the base
examples: Nutrients flow basipetally through the stem.
Flowers open basipetally on the raceme.

senses.definition: fear of falling or of walking, also known as astasia-abasia
examples: Patients with basiphobia avoid standing.
Basiphobia can result from neurological issues.

senses.definition: the basal joint or segment of the endopodite in crustacean limbs
examples: The basipodite articulates with the coxa.
Examine the basipodite in this shrimp appendage.

senses.definition: pertaining to the basipodite in arthropod anatomy
examples: Basipoditic structures vary by species.
The leg shows basipoditic features.

senses.definition: relating to the basal part of the pterygium in anatomy
examples: Basipterygial bones form the fin base.
The fossil had basipterygial extensions.

senses.definition: the proximal or basal bone of a pectoral fin in fish
examples: The basipterygium supports the fin rays.
Evolution of the basipterygium is studied in ichthyology.

senses.definition: relating to the processes extending from the base of the pterygoid bone in the skull
examples: Basipterygoid processes articulate with other bones.
The reptile skull features basipterygoid elements.

senses.definition: located at or relating to the base of a radial structure, as in echinoderms
examples: Basiradial plates form part of the ambulacrum.
The starfish arm has basiradial ossicles.

senses.definition: relating to the base of the nose or nasal region in anatomy
examples: Basirhinal measurements assess skull width.
The basirhinal line defines nasal dimensions.

senses.definition: pertaining to the base of the rostrum in anatomical structures
examples: Basirostral index measures beak proportions.
Bird skulls show basirostral features.

senses.definition: the main reason or idea that supports something
examples: There is no basis for that claim.
Friendship was the basis of their partnership.

senses.definition: the lowest part or foundation of something
examples: This rock forms the basis of the hill.
The basis of the column is wide.

senses.definition: facing or located on the side toward the base in ferns or plants
examples: Basiscopic veins branch toward the base.
The frond has basiscopic segments.

senses.definition: plural of basisidium, a spore-producing cell in basidiomycete fungi
examples: Basisidia line the gills of mushrooms.
Microscopy reveals basisidia with spores.

senses.definition: having a base that is free or unattached, as in certain leaves or cells
examples: Basisolute leaves detach easily at the base.
The petiole is basisolute in this species.
